name: Ash Tree
description: Yggdrasil, the World Tree, is the most prominent and sacred representation of the Ash Tree in Norse mythology. It connects the nine realms, its roots reaching deep into Hel and its branches touching Asgard. The Ash tree symbolizes life, knowledge, and the interconnectedness of all things. Its strength represents resilience and endurance, while its longevity mirrors the vastness of time. In rituals, ash wood was used for protection, divination, and connecting with the ancestral spirits. Runes were often carved into ash wood for spells and enchantments. The tree's leaves and bark were believed to possess healing properties, and were used in traditional Norse medicine. Beyond its practical uses, the Ash Tree held profound cultural significance, representing the very foundation of the Norse cosmos and the cyclical nature of existence. The first humans, Ask and Embla, were said to have been created from ash and elm trees, further cementing the Ash Tree's role as a symbol of humanity's origins and connection to the natural world. The fate of the cosmos is intertwined with Yggdrasil; its health reflects the well-being of the realms, and its eventual destruction signifies the end of the world, Ragnarok. Even after Ragnarok, life is destined to begin anew, suggesting the eternal cycle of life, death, and rebirth symbolized by the ash tree. The tree also provided practical resources such as wood for building and tools, and its leaves for animal fodder.
subtitle: Yggdrasil: The World Tree of Norse Myth

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Ask and Embla
keyMyths.description: Odin and his brothers Vili and Vé found two logs on the beach, an ash and an elm. From these logs, they created the first humans, Ask (ash) and Embla (elm).

keyMyths.title: Yggdrasil and the Nine Realms
keyMyths.description: Yggdrasil is the immense ash tree that connects the nine realms of Norse cosmology. Its roots extend into Hel, Jotunheim, and Niflheim, while its branches reach up to Asgard and other realms.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, containing stories and descriptions of Yggdrasil and other related concepts.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, containing stories and descriptions of Yggdrasil and other related concepts.

sources.author: Hilda Ellis Davidson
sources.work: Gods and Myths of Northern Europe
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Norse mythology, including the significance of trees and plants.
